intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard works better with simple, hand-drawn-style graphs than dense data visualizations
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward uncluttered, approachable data presentation with organic feel
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers either more sophisticated/polished charts or more detailed information density

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, approachable language over clinical tracking language when logging health experiments
facade.label: Casual check-in tone
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should feel like texting a friend; use contractions, informal language, and warmth in microcopy
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should maintain professional distance; use formal language, clinical terminology, and neutral tone throughout the interface

evidence.hypothesis: A health dashboard works better with simple, hand-drawn-style graphs than dense data visualizations
evidence.implication: User leans toward uncluttered, approachable data presentation with organic feel

facade.hypothesis: Users prioritize a single prominent metric (like weight or exercise streaks) over comparative or contextual data when scanning their dashboard
facade.axisTargeted: Information hierarchy—dominant metric vs. supporting details
facade.label: Big number, small context
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should lead with one key number per section, minimize surrounding data density, use visual dominance (size/color) to anchor attention
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should distribute information evenly across metrics, provide immediate context alongside numbers, use equal visual weight for related data points

facade.hypothesis: A calm, data-focused aesthetic using clean lines and generous whitespace will feel less clinical than a busy dashboard, supporting sustained engagement with health tracking
facade.axisTargeted: Warm vs Cool / Calm vs Energetic
facade.label: Minimal charts
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward understated, zen-like data visualization—values clarity over visual richness
facade.rejectImplies: User may want more personality, color, or visual warmth to make the tracking experience feel less sterile

facade.hypothesis: A dashboard with fluid, animated transitions between states feels more reflective and exploratory than instant, snappy state changes—and users will prefer it for long-term tracking where pacing and contemplation matter.
facade.axisTargeted: Animated fluid transitions vs instant snappy updates
facade.label: Smooth slide transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Emphasize motion: fade, slide, and ease animations when metrics update or views switch. Create a 'breathing' pace that invites lingering and reflection.
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant state changes with minimal or no transition. Let data appear immediately without delay. Crisp, direct updates feel more functional and lightweight.

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking multiple experiments need a persistent navigation anchor; a sidebar layout with experiment cards in the main area reduces cognitive load vs. tab-switching
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values stable navigation and clear information hierarchy; likely accepts dense, organized layouts with persistent context
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers horizontal navigation and/or flatter hierarchy; may want tab-based or card-only layouts without persistent sidebars

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard with dark mode reduces eye strain during frequent check-ins and creates a calming, focused environment for tracking personal experiments
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs Dark — visual comfort and mood
facade.label: Dark background, light text
facade.acceptImplies: User values reduced visual intensity and prefers nighttime-friendly interfaces; dashboard should default to or prominently feature dark mode
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers bright, high-contrast interfaces or associates dark mode with clinical/cold feel; light backgrounds may feel more approachable for health tracking

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
